frame_analyses.description: Setup view showing laparoscopic trainer with white discs and blue pegs. Timer at 04:00. Task not yet started.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Waiting for instruments to enter simulation area

frame_analyses.description: Both graspers visible in field. Left grasper approaching white disc (Penrose drain). Timer at 03:49.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instruments entering field, beginning needle loading phase

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper grasping white disc while right grasper approaches. Timer at 03:38.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper bimanual coordination, securing target for suturing

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments manipulating white disc. Blue suture material visible. Timer at 03:26.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Attempting to locate and grasp needle from suture material

frame_analyses.description: Right grasper has blue suture material. White disc held by left grasper. Timer at 03:15.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture material secured, working to load needle properly

frame_analyses.description: Needle loaded in right instrument. Beginning approach to white disc for suture placement. Timer at 03:04.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle appears properly loaded, transitioning to placement phase

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver penetrating through white disc. Left grasper stabilizing. Timer at 02:53.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good stabilization technique, needle passage appears controlled

frame_analyses.description: Needle passed through disc, blue suture visible on both sides. Timer at 02:42.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Successful needle passage, suture placement complete

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot tying sequence. Right grasper manipulating suture tail. Timer at 02:30.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initiating first throw of surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Active knot formation with both instruments. Complex suture manipulation visible. Timer at 02:19.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Working through double throw formation for surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Continuing knot sequence. Instruments working in different positions. Timer at 02:08.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transitioning to second throw, hand switching should occur

frame_analyses.description: Final stages of knot tying. Blue suture material prominently visible. Timer at 01:57.

confidence_rationale: Camera angle and distance make it difficult to assess suture placement accuracy relative to marks. Hand switching between throws cannot be clearly determined from the available frames. Knot completion and overall task completion are observable.
cannot_determine: Exact placement relative to marks on Penrose drain - marks not clearly visible
Hand switching between second and third throws - camera angle obscures detail
Precise suture deviation measurements - insufficient resolution of marking points
technique_summary: Novice-level performance with extended time in needle loading (56s vs 8s expert) and knot tying phases (144s vs 40s expert). Task completed successfully with apparent knot security.
strengths: Good bimanual coordination during suture placement
Appears to maintain proper instrument control throughout
improvement_suggestions: Practice needle loading to reduce time from 56s to under 20s
Work on knot tying efficiency - 144s for three throws is significantly above benchmark
Focus on hand switching technique during knot sequence
